strengths: Clear problem framing: truth maintenance in long-horizon agent memory
Proposes a novel structured memory graph with explicit temporal and contradiction edges
Reports strong gains on temporal-reasoning slice (+40pp) and efficiency improvements
Includes ablation study isolating the contribution of the linker mechanism
weaknesses: Overall accuracy on LongMemEval-S is lower than flat baseline (47.4% vs 63.2%)
Method details are high-level; lacks algorithmic pseudocode and formal definitions
Evaluation is limited to a single primary benchmark (LongMemEval-S) with small subset sizes
No statistical significance testing or error bars reported for results
Placeholder numbers and macros throughout the paper undermine credibility
required_revisions: Replace all placeholder macros (e.g., \FlatLMEAcc, \DeltaLMETR) with actual numeric results
Provide full algorithm pseudocode for the maximum-consistent subgraph retrieval
Include statistical significance tests and confidence intervals for reported improvements
Clarify the claim extraction process and how SPO triples are obtained
Add a formal definition of the claim schema and edge types
Report results on the full LongMemEval benchmark, not just the -S subset
Include a comparison to more recent memory baselines beyond A-Mem

strengths: Clear problem framing: truth maintenance for long-horizon agents
Proposes a novel structured memory schema with explicit temporal edges
Reports strong gains on target temporal-reasoning slice
Includes efficiency analysis showing reduced token cost
Provides detailed ablation isolating contribution of truth-maintenance edges
weaknesses: Overall accuracy on primary benchmark is lower than simple baseline
No statistical significance testing reported for results
Ablation coverage incomplete (e.g., no ablation of temporal parser)
Small sample sizes for key slices (KU N=3, ABS N=1) limit confidence
Cross-domain evaluation on LoCoMo/MemoryAgentBench not reported quantitatively
required_revisions: Report standard deviations or confidence intervals for all accuracy numbers
Conduct statistical significance tests comparing TTMG to baselines
Provide full ablation study including temporal parser and maximum-consistent-subgraph components
Report quantitative results for cross-domain evaluations (LoCoMo, MemoryAgentBench)
Clarify sample sizes for all reported metrics in tables
Address the performance drop on non-temporal slices in full TTMG

strengths: Clear problem framing: truth maintenance for long-horizon agents
Novel integration of truth maintenance with graph-based memory
Strong empirical gains on temporal-reasoning slice
Significant token and latency reduction vs. baseline
Ablation study isolates contribution of key mechanism
weaknesses: Overall accuracy lower than flat baseline (trade-off not fully justified)
Placeholder results (e.g., N=3 for knowledge-update) limit confidence
No discussion of system robustness to noisy or ambiguous claims
Scalability of maximum-consistent-subgraph on larger k not analyzed
Cross-domain results (LoCoMo, MemoryAgentBench) only mentioned, not shown
required_revisions: Replace all placeholder macros (e.g., \FlatLMEAcc, \DeltaLMETR) with actual numeric results in tables and text.
Provide concrete results for LoCoMo and MemoryAgentBench cross-domain evaluations.
Clarify the trade-off: why is lower overall accuracy acceptable? Discuss deployment implications.
Add analysis of computational complexity and memory overhead of the graph structure.
Discuss failure modes: what happens when the LLM linker mislabels edges?
